name: Kindergarten Martial Arts — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms through a close look at respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms through a guided practice with respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms through a small creation or demonstration about respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms.

units.title: Wonder and notice: respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in respect, ready stance, balance, and non-contact movement forms.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
